title: dryadodaphne novoguineensis var. macra
intro: Dryadodaphne novoguineensis var. macra, also known as the dryadodaphne, is a plant species that belongs to the laurel family (Lauraceae) and is native to New Guinea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of dryadodaphne include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Dryadodaphne can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and its essential oil can also be extracted for medicinal use.
